name: Create Custom Helm Charts
description: Scaffold, structure, and build custom Helm charts for your applications with proper templates, helpers, and values files.
trendingReason: Teams increasingly package internal services as Helm charts for standardized deployments

steps.title: Scaffold a new chart
steps.description: Generate the default Helm chart directory structure.
steps.tip: The scaffold includes a Deployment, Service, ServiceAccount, Ingress, and HPA template ready to customize.

steps.title: Explore the chart structure
steps.description: Understand the key files and directories in the generated chart.

steps.title: Edit the values file
steps.description: Customize the default values that will be used across your templates.
steps.tip: Keep values.yaml well-documented with comments explaining each parameter and its default.

steps.title: Add a custom template
steps.description: Create a ConfigMap template using Helm template syntax.
steps.command:
cat > myapp/templates/configmap.yaml << 'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: {{ include "myapp.fullname" . }}-config
  labels:
    {{- include "myapp.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
data:
  APP_ENV: {{ .Values.config.environment | quote }}
  LOG_LEVEL: {{ .Values.config.logLevel | quote }}
EOF

steps.title: Render templates locally
steps.description: Preview the generated Kubernetes manifests without deploying.
steps.command: helm template my-release myapp --set config.environment=production --set config.logLevel=warn

steps.title: Package the chart
steps.description: Create a versioned .tgz archive of the chart for distribution.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Helm Chart Creation Workflow
set -euo pipefail

CHART_NAME=${1:?"Usage: $0 <chart-name>"}

echo "=== Scaffolding Chart ==="
helm create "$CHART_NAME"

echo ""
echo "=== Chart Structure ==="
find "$CHART_NAME" -type f | sort

echo ""
echo "=== Adding ConfigMap Template ==="
cat > "$CHART_NAME/templates/configmap.yaml" << 'TMPL'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: {{ include "CHART.fullname" . }}-config
  labels:
    {{- include "CHART.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
data:
  APP_ENV: {{ .Values.config.environment | default "development" | quote }}
  LOG_LEVEL: {{ .Values.config.logLevel | default "info" | quote }}
TMPL

# Replace CHART placeholder with actual chart name
sed -i "s/CHART/$CHART_NAME/g" "$CHART_NAME/templates/configmap.yaml"

echo ""
echo "=== Rendering Templates ==="
helm template test-release "$CHART_NAME"

echo ""
echo "=== Linting Chart ==="
helm lint "$CHART_NAME"

echo ""
echo "=== Packaging Chart ==="
helm package "$CHART_NAME"

echo "Done! Chart packaged as $CHART_NAME-0.1.0.tgz"

faq.question: How do I add dependencies to my chart?
faq.answer: Define dependencies in Chart.yaml under the 'dependencies' key with name, version, and repository. Then run 'helm dependency update' to download them into the charts/ directory.

faq.question: What are named templates and when should I use them?
faq.answer: Named templates (defined in _helpers.tpl with 'define') are reusable template snippets. Use them for labels, selectors, and resource names that appear in multiple templates to keep your chart DRY.

faq.question: How do I handle secrets in Helm charts?
faq.answer: Never commit secrets in values.yaml. Use Kubernetes Secrets with values injected at deploy time via --set or external secret managers like External Secrets Operator or Sealed Secrets.
